title: Percent, Rates, Markups, Discounts, and Repeated Change · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Audit prices, discounts, taxes, tips, population changes, and performance claims. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a consumer percent-claim audit after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify the comparison base before calculating a percent, percentage, or whole.
Calculate absolute and relative change and explain when each comparison is useful.
Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

lines.label: assumption

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Percent Claims, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Percent Claims applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Detect denominator switching, percentage-point confusion, and selective baselines in public claims.
